results.Description: THE TULALIP TRIBAL PD PROPOSES AN INNOVATIVE PROJECT TO RESHAPE PUBLIC PERCEPTION OF THE TULALIP POLICE DEPARTMENT BY HIGHLIGHTING THE HUMAN SIDE OF POLICING AND THE DEPARTMENT'S COMMITMENT TO SERVICE, RESPECT, AND CULTURAL UNDERSTANDING. THROUGH COMPELLING STORYTELLING, PROFESSIONAL MEDIA CONTENT, AND TARGETED OUTREACH, THIS PROGRAM WILL TO STRENGTHEN TRUST, PROMOTE TRANSPARENCY, AND INSPIRE FUTURE GENERATIONS OF TRIBAL YOUTH TO CONSIDER CAREERS IN LAW ENFORCEMENT.

THE RIDGEFIELD POLICE DEPARTMENT, A RURAL AGENCY IN SOUTHWEST WASHINGTON, PROPOSES A NEW, MULTIFACETED RECRUITMENT PROJECT TO ATTRACT MORE CANDIDATES TO THEIR GROWING POLICE DEPARTMENT. THE PROJECT WILL INCLUDE DIGITAL RECRUITMENT STRATEGIES SUCH AS PRODUCING RECRUITMENT VIDEOS, A RECRUITMENT-SPECIFIC WEBSITE, AND TARGETED SOCIAL MEDIA ADVERTISING. ADDITIONALLY, CURRENT POLICE OFFICERS WILL ATTEND COMMUNITY AND HIGHER EDUCATION RECRUITMENT EVENTS IN WASHINGTON AND OREGON IN ORDER TO PROVIDE PERSONAL OUTREACH TO POTENTIAL CANDIDATES. PROJECT DELIVERABLES WILL INCLUDE SHAREABLE VIDEOS, A WEBSITE, RECRUITMENT PAMPHLETS, AND A REPORT THAT INCLUDES KEY TAKEAWAYS AND LESSONS LEARNED, ALL OF WHICH CAN HELP OTHER RURAL POLICE DEPARTMENTS IN THEIRRECRUITMENT EFFORTS.

THE EVERETT POLICE DEPARTMENT (EPD) PRIMARY GOAL OF THIS PROJECT IS TO PROVIDE OFFICERS WITH REALISTIC, IMMERSIVE TRAINING THAT PREPARES THEM TO ENGAGE INDIVIDUALS IN CRISIS WITH EMPATHY, PROFESSIONALISM, AND CULTURAL COMPETENCE. THE TRAINING WILL FOCUS ON IMPROVING OFFICER DECISION-MAKING AND COMMUNICATION WHEN INTERACTING WITH INDIVIDUALS EXPERIENCING MENTAL HEALTH CRISES, BEHAVIORAL HEALTH CHALLENGES, SUICIDAL IDEATION, OR COGNITIVE AND DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES. EPD PROVIDES TRAINING SUPPORT TO 11 ADDITIONAL LAW ENFORCEMENT AGENCIES.

THE PURPOSE OF THE COPS OFFICE TRIBAL RESOURCES GRANT EQUIPMENT/TRAINING PROGRAM (TRGP-EQUIPMENT/TRAINING) IS TO ADVANCE THE PRACTICE OF COMMUNITY POLICING IN TRIBAL JURISDICTIONS AND MEET THE MOST SERIOUS TRIBAL LAW ENFORCEMENT NEEDS. FUNDING UNDER THIS AWARD PROGRAM WILL BE UTILIZED BY THE TRIBAL JURISDICTION TO PROCURE BASIC EQUIPMENT, TECHNOLOGY AND TRAINING TO INCREASE THE JURISDICTIONS COMMUNITY POLICING CAPACITY TO PREVENT AND DISRUPT CRIME AND VIOLENCE.

results.Description: THE PURPOSE OF THE COPS OFFICE TRIBAL RESOURCES GRANT EQUIPMENT/TRAINING PROGRAM (TRGP-EQUIPMENT/TRAINING) IS TO ADVANCE THE PRACTICE OF COMMUNITY POLICING IN TRIBAL JURISDICTIONS AND MEET THE MOST SERIOUS TRIBAL LAW ENFORCEMENT NEEDS. FUNDING UNDER THIS AWARD PROGRAM WILL BE UTILIZED BY THE TRIBAL JURISDICTION TO PROCURE BASIC EQUIPMENT, TECHNOLOGY AND TRAINING TO INCREASE THE JURISDICTIONS COMMUNITY POLICING CAPACITY TO PREVENT AND DISRUPT CRIME AND VIOLENCE.

THE PURPOSE OF THE COPS OFFICE TRIBAL RESOURCES GRANT HIRING PROGRAM (TRGP-HIRE) IS TO ADVANCE THE PRACTICE OF COMMUNITY POLICING IN TRIBAL JURISDICTIONS THROUGH THE HIRE OR REHIRE OF ADDITIONAL CAREER SWORN LAW ENFORCEMENT OFFICERS, TRIBAL/VILLAGE POLICE OFFICERS, AND VILLAGE PUBLIC SAFETY OFFICERS. FUNDING UNDER THIS AWARD PROGRAM WILL BE UTILIZED BY THE TRIBAL JURISDICTION TO HIRE AND REHIRE CAREER SWORN LAW ENFORCEMENT OFFICERS, TRIBAL/VILLAGE POLICE OFFICERS, AND VILLAGE PUBLIC SAFETY OFFICERS NECESSARY TO INCREASE THE JURISDICTIONS COMMUNITY POLICING CAPACITY TO PREVENT AND DISRUPT CRIME AND VIOLENCE.

THE SPOKANE COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE (SCSO) WILL USE FY24 SAFER OUTCOMES TO CARRY OUT MOTIVATIONAL INTERVIEWING (MI) TRAINING PROVIDED BY THE INSTITUTE FOR INDIVIDUAL AND ORGANIZATIONAL CHANGE (IFIOC) AND TACTICAL SOCIAL INTERACTIONS (TSI) DEVELOPED FOR THE DEFENSE ADVANCED RESEARCH PROJECTS AGENCY (DARPA). THIS PROJECT WILL ALLOW 24 SCSO FIELD TRAINING OFFICERS AND 18 FIRST-LINE SUPERVISORS TO PARTICIPATE IN FOUR TWO-DAY BLOCKS OF MI TRAINING FOR LAW ENFORCEMENT, AS WELL AS TWO ONE-WEEK TRAININGS OF TSI. SCSO HAS APPROXIMATELY 230 SWORN DEPUTIES, SERVES A POPULATION OF 550,000 PEOPLE, AND IS THE PRIMARY POLICE AGENCY FOR NINE CONTRACT CITIES. THE DEPARTMENT IS FOCUSED ON EVIDENCE-BASED STRATEGIES TO ENHANCE POLICE-COMMUNITY RELATIONS AND SAFETY.

PROJECT TASKS INCLUDE THE IMPLEMENTATION OF FOUR TWO-DAY MI TRAININGS AND TWO FORTY-HOUR TSI TRAININGS FOR SCSO DEPUTIES AND SUPERVISORS AND A PROGRAM EVALUATION. THE SUCCESS OF THE MI AND TSI TRAINING PROJECT IS SUPPORTED BY KEY PARTNERSHIPS THAT COMBINE EXPERTISE FROM VARIOUS FIELDS. SCSO'S MENTAL HEALTH PROFESSIONAL WILL SUPPORT THE INTEGRATION OF TRAINING TO ENSURE PSYCHOLOGICAL PRINCIPLES ARE INCLUDED AND THAT TRAINING DELIVERY WILL GAIN BUY-IN AMONGST SCSO DEPUTIES. SCSO WORKS WITH POLISSOLUTIONS, UTILIZING THEIR TRUSTSTAT TECHNOLOGY, WHICH ANALYZES BWC FOOTAGE USING AI, NATURAL LANGUAGE PROCESSING, AND COMPUTER VISION. BWC REVIEW WILL BE USED TO UNDERSTAND THE IMPACT OF THE TRAINING ON POLICE BEHAVIOR IN THE FIELD AND FOR TRAINING PURPOSES TO OPTIMIZE PERFORMANCE. LASTLY, SCSO WILL PARTNER WITH PARKS RESEARCH LLC TO EVALUATE AND CONTINUOUSLY IMPROVE THE PROGRAM THROUGH A MIXED-METHODS STUDY EXAMINING THE IMPACT OF TRAINING ON INTENDED OUTCOMES. THIS WILL CONTRIBUTE TO EVIDENCE-BASED POLICING RESEARCH, PARTICULARLY THROUGH THE DISSEMINATION OF RESEARCH DELIVERABLES.

KEY PROJECT DELIVERABLES INCLUDE A DETAILED SCHEDULE AND REPORT ON TOTAL NUMBER OF TRAINING SESSIONS CONDUCTED FOR SCSO DEPUTIES, A THOROUGH DATA ANALYSIS/EVALUATION OF THE PROGRAM, DETAILED PRESENTATIONS, AND WRITTEN REPORTS SUMMARIZING TRAINING OUTCOMES AND KEY FINDINGS. IF SUPPORTED BY EVALUATION RESULTS, SCSO WILL DEVELOP AND IMPLEMENT POLICIES OR UPDATE EXISTING ONES TO INTEGRATE MI AS A DE-ESCALATION PRACTICE WITHIN TRAINING PROGRAMS. THIS PROJECT REPRESENTS A COMPREHENSIVE AND INNOVATIVE APPROACH TO PROMOTE SAFER AND MORE EFFECTIVE POLICING, WITH THE POTENTIAL TO UPDATE WIDESPREAD POLICIES AND TRAINING STANDARDS BASED ON OUTCOMES OF THE TRAINING.

THE STEILACOOM DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC SAFETY (SDPS) WILL USE FY24 SAFER OUTCOMES FUNDING TO ACQUIRE STREET SMARTS VIRTUAL REALITY TRAINING SYSTEMS. SERVING IN A COUNTY WITH MANY SMALLER JURISDICTIONS, WE ARE PLEASED TO EXPAND TRAINING TO POLICE DEPARTMENTS IN THE CITIES AND TOWNS OF DUPONT, SUMNER, BUCKLEY, PUYALLUP, LAKEWOOD, EDGEWOOD, UNIVERSITY PLACE, BONNEY LAKE, EATONVILLE, FIFE, FIRCREST, GIG HARBOR, MILTON, ORTING, PACIFIC, ROY, AND RUSTON.   THE STREET SMARTS VR STATE-OF-THE-ART TOOLS WILL OFFER OUR POLICE DEPARTMENT, AND OTHER POLICE DEPARTMENTS IN OUR COUNTY, IMMERSIVE, SCENARIO-BASED EXERCISES THAT MIMIC REAL-LIFE SITUATIONS, GREATLY ENHANCING TRAINING CAPABILITIES. WITH VR SYSTEMS, OFFICERS WILL BE ABLE TO PRACTICE AND HONE THEIR RESPONSES TO CRITICAL INCIDENTS, INCLUDING ACTIVE SHOOTER SITUATIONS, DOMESTIC VIOLENCE, SERVING WARRANTS, AND OTHER DANGEROUS POLICE SITUATIONS. THIS COMPREHENSIVE TRAINING APPROACH INCLUDES FOLLOW-UP ASSESSMENTS TO EVALUATE KNOWLEDGE RETENTION AND THE APPLICATION OF SKILLS IN REAL-WORLD SETTINGS, ENSURING LONG-TERM EFFECTIVENESS. FUNDING WILL COVER THE PURCHASE OF THE VR SYSTEMS, STEILACOOM OFFICER TRAINING, AND SENDING AN OFFICER TO TRAIN EACH OF THE 17 COLLABORATIVE AGENCIES TWICE A YEAR FOR 2 YEARS TO TRAIN THEIR OFFICERS.

THE COPS HIRING PROGRAM (CHP) PROVIDES FUNDING TO LAW ENFORCEMENT AGENCIES TO HIRE AND/OR REHIRE ADDITIONAL CAREER LAW ENFORCEMENT OFFICERS IN AN EFFORT TO INCREASE THEIR COMMUNITY POLICING CAPACITY AND CRIME PREVENTION EFFORTS. ANTICIPATED OUTCOMES OF CHP AWARDS INCLUDE ENGAGEMENT IN PLANNED COMMUNITY PARTNERSHIPS, IMPLEMENTATION OF PROJECTS TO ANALYZE AND ASSESS PROBLEMS, IMPLEMENTATION OF CHANGES TO PERSONNEL AND AGENCY MANAGEMENT IN SUPPORT OF COMMUNITY POLICING, AND INCREASED CAPACITY OF AGENCY TO ENGAGE IN COMMUNITY POLICING ACTIVITIES.

THE PURPOSE OF THE COPS OFFICE SCHOOL VIOLENCE PREVENTION PROGRAM (SVPP) IS TO IMPROVE SECURITY AT SCHOOLS AND ON SCHOOL GROUNDS THROUGH SCHOOL SAFETY PROGRAMS AND TECHNOLOGY. FUNDING UNDER THIS AWARD PROGRAM WILL BE USED BY THE SCHOOL JURISDICTION TO IMPLEMENT EVIDENCE-BASED PROGRAMS TO IMPROVE SCHOOL SECURITY AND PROMOTE A POSITIVE LEARNING ENVIRONMENT FOR ALL STUDENTS.

THE COPS HIRING PROGRAM (CHP) PROVIDES FUNDING TO LAW ENFORCEMENT AGENCIES TO HIRE AND/OR REHIRE ADDITIONAL CAREER LAW ENFORCEMENT OFFICERS IN AN EFFORT TO INCREASE THEIR COMMUNITY POLICING CAPACITY AND CRIME PREVENTION EFFORTS. ANTICIPATED OUTCOMES OF CHP AWARDS INCLUDE ENGAGEMENT IN PLANNED COMMUNITY PARTNERSHIPS, IMPLEMENTATION OF PROJECTS TO ANALYZE AND ASSESS PROBLEMS, IMPLEMENTATION OF CHANGES TO PERSONNEL AND AGENCY MANAGEMENT IN SUPPORT OF COMMUNITY POLICING, AND INCREASED CAPACITY OF AGENCY TO ENGAGE IN COMMUNITY POLICING ACTIVITIES.

THE FY24 COPS OFFICE TECHNOLOGY AND EQUIPMENT PROGRAM (TEP) PROVIDES GRANTS TO STATE, LOCAL, TRIBAL, TERRITORIAL, AND OTHER ENTITIES TO DEVELOP AND ACQUIRE EFFECTIVE EQUIPMENT, TECHNOLOGIES, AND INTEROPERABLE COMMUNICATIONS THAT ASSIST IN RESPONDING TO AND PREVENTING CRIME.

THE GOAL OF THE PROGRAM IS TO INCREASE THE COMMUNITY POLICING CAPACITY AND CRIME PREVENTION EFFORTS OF LAW ENFORCEMENT AGENCIES. THE OBJECTIVE IS TO PROVIDE FUNDING FOR PROJECTS WHICH IMPROVE POLICE EFFECTIVENESS AND THE FLOW OF INFORMATION AMONG LAW ENFORCEMENT AGENCIES, LOCAL GOVERNMENT SERVICE PROVIDERS, AND THE COMMUNITIES THEY SERVE. FUNDING SHALL BE USED FOR THE PROJECTS, AND IN THE AMOUNTS, SPECIFIED UNDER THE HEADING COMMUNITY ORIENTED POLICING SERVICES, TECHNOLOGY AND EQUIPMENT COMMUNITY PROJECTS/COPS LAW ENFORCEMENT TECHNOLOGY AND EQUIPMENT IN CONGRESSIONAL JOINT EXPLANATORY STATEMENT  DIVISION C, WHICH IS INCORPORATED BY REFERENCE INTO PUBLIC LAW 118-42.

messages: For searches, time period start and end dates are currently limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01.  For data going back to 2000-10-01, use either the Custom Award Download feature on the website or one of our download or bulk_download API endpoints as listed on https://api.usaspending.gov/docs/endpoints.
